question: Over motor fuel revenue years 2013-2023, which state (excluding DC) has the 24th-highest average gasoline tax rate? Among municipal candidates in the most populous city of that state who filed campaign expenditures in all five election cycles (2001, 2003, 2005, 2009, 2013) with between $103,000 and $251,000 in each cycle, find the candidate who represents the borough that recorded the highest number of housing authority residents enrolled in the 2019 Summer Youth Employment Program. Which candidate is it? Only report the candidate name in `First Last` format. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact: Across MMFR years 2013-2023, New York's average gasoline tax rate is 25.5045, which is the 24th-highest among states (excluding DC and Federal Tax) in the motor fuel tax dataset.
1.subquestion: Across MMFR years 2013-2023, which state (excluding DC and Federal Tax) has the 24th-highest average gasoline tax rate in the motor fuel tax dataset?

2.fact: New York City is the most populous city in the United States and is located at the southern tip of New York State.
2.subquestion: What city is located at the southern tip of <node_1 answer> and is the most populous city in the United States?

3.fact: In the 2001 NYC election cycle, 105 municipal candidates filed campaign expenditures between $103,000 and $251,000. This includes Leroy Comrie ($162,364) and Domenic Recchia ($198,481).
3.subquestion: Which municipal candidates in <node_2 answer> filed campaign expenditures between $103,000 and $251,000 in the 2001 election cycle? (Filter: sum AMNT by candidate (CANDID, CANDFIRST, CANDLAST); keep totals between $103,000 and $251,000.)

4.fact: In the 2003 NYC election cycle, 47 municipal candidates filed campaign expenditures between $103,000 and $251,000. This includes Leroy Comrie ($199,092) and Domenic Recchia ($147,522).
4.subquestion: Which municipal candidates in <node_2 answer> filed campaign expenditures between $103,000 and $251,000 in the 2003 election cycle? (Filter: sum AMNT by candidate (CANDID, CANDFIRST, CANDLAST); keep totals between $103,000 and $251,000.)

5.fact: In the 2005 NYC election cycle, 69 municipal candidates filed campaign expenditures between $103,000 and $251,000. This includes Leroy Comrie ($196,379) and Domenic Recchia ($184,213).
5.subquestion: Which municipal candidates in <node_2 answer> filed campaign expenditures between $103,000 and $251,000 in the 2005 election cycle? (Filter: sum AMNT by candidate (CANDID, CANDFIRST, CANDLAST); keep totals between $103,000 and $251,000.)

6.fact: In the 2009 NYC election cycle, 99 municipal candidates filed campaign expenditures between $103,000 and $251,000. This includes Leroy Comrie ($250,436.07) and Domenic Recchia ($103,429.22).
6.subquestion: Which municipal candidates in <node_2 answer> filed campaign expenditures between $103,000 and $251,000 in the 2009 election cycle? (Filter: sum AMNT by candidate (CANDID, CANDFIRST, CANDLAST); keep totals between $103,000 and $251,000.)

7.fact: In the 2013 NYC election cycle, 93 municipal candidates filed campaign expenditures between $103,000 and $251,000. This includes Leroy Comrie ($128,205.60) and Domenic Recchia ($232,908.02).
7.subquestion: Which municipal candidates in <node_2 answer> filed campaign expenditures between $103,000 and $251,000 in the 2013 election cycle? (Filter: sum AMNT by candidate (CANDID, CANDFIRST, CANDLAST); keep totals between $103,000 and $251,000.)

8.fact: Leroy George Comrie Jr. represents district 14 in the New York State Senate, which comprises neighborhoods within the borough of Queens.
8.subquestion: Which borough in <node_2 answer> did Leroy Comrie (from <intersection of nodes 3-7>) represent?

9.fact: Domenic Michael Recchia Jr. formerly represented the 47th district of the New York City Council, which included areas of Bensonhurst, Brighton Beach, Coney Island, and Gravesend in south Brooklyn.
9.subquestion: Which borough in <node_2 answer> did Domenic Recchia (from <intersection of nodes 3-7>) represent?

10.fact: In 2019, Brooklyn had 3,889 NYCHA residents enrolled in SYEP while Queens had 1,019 enrollees. Brooklyn (3,889) had more enrollees than Queens (1,019).
10.subquestion: Which borough had more NYCHA residents enrolled in SYEP in 2019: Queens (<node_8 answer>) or Brooklyn (<node_9 answer>)? (Filter: Year == 2019; compare 'Were accepted and enrolled' for Queens vs Brooklyn.)

reasoning_chain: Node 1: Motor fuel tax dataset -> 2013-2023 average gasoline rate ranks 24th highest among states (excluding DC and Federal Tax) -> New York
Node 2: NYC page -> most populous U.S. city located at the southern tip of New York State -> New York City
Node 3: 2001 expenditures $103K-$251K -> 105 candidates (answer abbreviated: Recchia, Comrie, +103 others)
Node 4: 2003 expenditures $103K-$251K -> 47 candidates (answer abbreviated: Recchia, Comrie, +45 others)
Node 5: 2005 expenditures $103K-$251K -> 69 candidates (answer abbreviated: Recchia, Comrie, +67 others)
Node 6: 2009 expenditures $103K-$251K -> 99 candidates (answer abbreviated: Recchia, Comrie, +97 others)
Node 7: 2013 expenditures $103K-$251K -> 93 candidates (answer abbreviated: Recchia, Comrie, +91 others)
Node 8: Leroy Comrie -> borough of Queens
Node 9: Domenic Recchia -> borough of Brooklyn
Node 10: NYCHA SYEP 2019 enrollment -> Brooklyn (3,889) vs Queens (1,019)
Comparative: Brooklyn has more -> Domenic Recchia
